$comment: Expected canonical post-migration state for docs/save_compat/manifest.json's c3-typed-reference-v1-multipage fixture (issue #766 requirement 11: 'Include multi-page ... coverage', round-4 review). Byte-identical page1 content to c3-typed-reference-v1-minimal (same real cargo_hold_S/acolyte/forge_steel_dagger, extracted via decodeSessionEnvelope then re-encoded via encodeSessionSnapshot with a SECOND page added: 'page2', a deliberately empty page -- no entities, so no building/unit id needs a fresh unique allocation across pages -- with its own distinct clock (08:30 vs page1's 12:00) proving each page's own PageSnapshot fields (clock, map mode, entity slices) are captured, assembled, and reassembled independently, not merged or confused across pages. visiblePages lists both; activePage stays 'page1'. This is what proves the manifest-driven hspec gate and the fresh-process migration probe's structural assertions genuinely iterate every page in snapPages, not just the first/only one every other tracked fixture happens to have.
